items.title: Tauri 1.0 ships with stable plugin system
items.summary: Tauri released version 1.0 with a production-ready plugin ecosystem for building native desktop apps with web tech. The plugin system allows reusable capabilities across projects.
items.why_it_matters: Desktop app development with web skills is becoming more accessible. This is relevant if you ever need to ship a local tool that talks to your file system or system APIs without the overhead of Electron.

takes.take: Notes the ecosystem maturity and adoption in production apps.

items.title: Rust stabilizes cargo script for single-file programs
items.summary: Cargo now supports a stable `cargo-script` feature for running single-file Rust programs without a full project setup. Reduces friction for scripting use cases.
items.why_it_matters: Lowers the barrier to writing quick Rust scripts. If you ever need glue code or a one-off utility with Rust's safety guarantees, this makes it nearly as quick as Python.

items.title: DeepSeek releases R1 reasoning model open-source
items.summary: DeepSeek unveiled R1, an open-source reasoning model that mirrors some capabilities of proprietary reasoning systems. Weights available on Hugging Face.
items.why_it_matters: Open reasoning models expand the toolkit for local experimentation and fine-tuning. Relevant for understanding how reasoning architectures work without paying per-token fees.

items.title: Anthropic increases free-tier API rate limits
items.summary: Anthropic bumped free-tier rate limits on the API to 50K tokens/minute, opening more experimental capacity for hobbyists.
items.why_it_matters: Makes tinkering with Claude on the free tier less frustrating. Useful if you're testing batch workflows or building a side project.
